cross_page_patterns.description: Stylized callout banner text 'コレが出る！' consistently OCR'd as 'つしが出る！' or 'つしか出る！'. Confirmed on pages 217, 311, 314. Likely systemic across all pages containing this decorative element.
cross_page_patterns.recommended_fix: Add a global OCR post-correction rule: regex replace /試験にはつし[かが]出る/g → '試験にはコレが出る！' across all ocr/*.md files. Then re-run cleaning/structuring pipeline if callout content is ever extracted.

cross_page_patterns.description: Blockquote markdown in OCR output escaped as '>' HTML entities instead of raw '>' on page 314.
cross_page_patterns.recommended_fix: Unescape > in OCR markdown post-processing step.

cross_page_patterns.description: Difficulty/level icon rendered as lone 'A' character on its own line (page 240).
cross_page_patterns.recommended_fix: Strip isolated single uppercase ASCII letters on their own line in OCR output when surrounded by Japanese text and image refs.

overall_assessment: No FAIL verdicts. Zero release-impacting defects across all 8 pages. All findings are confined to OCR narrative text and do not propagate to structured translated output or release pages. The dominant systemic issue is the recurring callout banner misread ('コレが' → 'つしが'/'つしか') which warrants a corpus-wide fix in the OCR post-correction pipeline but does not affect end-user-visible content in v1.0.0.
